tldr: This quarter the cohort executed a clear rotation: out of mega-cap software (MSFT led all sells at 5 managers) and consumer/healthcare defensives (UNH, TGT, CMG), and into semiconductors/AI infrastructure (SMH ETF initiated by 3, plus AVGO, MU, SNDK, TSM) and quality financials/payments (V bought by 4, SPGI by 3, MCO by 2). Mega-cap internet (GOOGL, AMZN) saw the most two-way activity — still among the most-owned names at 11 managers each but also trimmed by 3-4, suggesting profit-taking rather than abandonment. Industrial/infrastructure names (DNOW, SUNB) saw quiet multi-manager initiations.

themes.heading: Rotating from mega-cap software into semis & AI infrastructure
themes.detail: MSFT was the most-sold name (5 managers) while the group initiated or added to semiconductor exposure across the stack — SMH ETF (3 initiations), AVGO and SNDK and MU (2 initiations each), and TSM (3 adds). This reads as a deliberate shift from software mega-caps into the AI hardware/picks-and-shovels layer.

themes.heading: Quality financials & payments as a consensus overweight
themes.detail: Visa was bought by 4 managers (2 initiations) and is now held by 9, while S&P Global (3 adds) and Moody's (2 adds) also saw accumulation. SLM (2 initiations) added a credit angle. The group is leaning into high-margin, duopoly/oligopoly financial infrastructure rather than complex alternatives like Brookfield (sold by 3) or Rocket (sold by 3).

themes.heading: De-risking consumer discretionary & healthcare
themes.detail: UnitedHealth (4 sellers), Target (3), Chipotle (3), and MercadoLibre (3) were all trimmed or exited, suggesting reduced appetite for consumer-facing and managed-care names. The cohort appears to be reducing exposure to both domestic retail (TGT, CMG) and LatAm e-commerce (MELI) while healthcare leadership (UNH) fell out of favor.

themes.heading: Mega-cap internet: two-way but still core
themes.detail: GOOGL and GOOG (each held by 11 managers) and AMZN (held by 11) saw both meaningful adds (GOOGL/GOOG bought by 3 each, AMZN by 4) and meaningful trims (GOOGL sold by 3, AMZN by 4). The net signal is profit-taking at the position level rather than a directional call — these remain the cohort's most widely held positions.
